content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: Staff

Date: Nov. 1, 1978

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Natalie West

Subject: Amendments to the Act: effective date

A number of statutory amendments will take effect January 1, 1979. A variety of questions have arisen concerning the application of new standards to events occurring before that date. Unless amended at the November 6, opinion request meeting, the following represents the staff consensus:

I. Statements of Economic Interest:

A. Annual Statements - If filed in 1979, the disclosure should conform to standards effective in 1979 (i.e., new legislation) even though the period covered is calendar year 1978. The only exception is Section 87313 which requires disclosure by and of intermediaries who make gifts.

B. Assuming office statements - If filed in 1979, apply new standards even though the office was assumed in 1978.

II. Campaign Statements:

A. Post election statement for the November election will be filed in 1979 but covers a period ending December 31, 1978. Apply the standards that existed during the period covered (i.e., before effective date of amendments) to avoid changing rules in midstream.

B. Off-year statements covering period ending December 31, 1978, filed in January 1979. Apply standards that existed in 1978 (i.e., $50).

C. Local elections - Some of the transactions disclosed in campaign statements filed in the March - April local elections will have occurred in 1978. Nevertheless, the campaign statement should comply with the standards established by the amendments (i.e., $100).

Staff agreed to comply with standards set forth in the memo.

Natalie
